extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMANN Pascal won from reduced bunch sprint after KWIATKOWSKI Michał crashed 2km out while in yellow, losing jersey to IMPEY Daryl via bonus seconds.
tactical_patterns: Mitchelton-Scott seized peloton control on Montée de l'Avocat descent, demonstrating breakaway-catching expertise on technical downhills under one minute.
ACKERMANN switched sprint positioning from front to back-of-pack free-wheeling approach after stage 1 mistake, enabling higher entry speed.
Bonus seconds proved decisive when KWIATKOWSKI crashed inside 3km protection zone, tactical argument for sprint train dominance on hilly sprint stages.

rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: deliberate tactical adjustment from frontal to rear sprint positioning after prior stage error shows adaptive intelligence
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: negative: lost yellow jersey via bonus seconds despite 3km rule protection after roundabout crash, positioning vulnerability exposed
rider_signals.IMPEY Daryl: positive: third place finish sufficient for yellow via 4-second bonus when rival crashed, demonstrates bonus-hunting awareness
rider_signals.PÉRICHON Pierre-Luc: positive: swept all four climbing bonuses from breakaway, revealing KOM jersey hunting strategy
rider_signals.FEILLU Brice: negative: polka dot jersey dropped on Montée de l'Avocat despite early climb points, insufficient threshold for hilly sprint stages
rider_signals.COQUARD Bryan: negative: dropped on Montée de l'Avocat alongside FEILLU, signals BMC sprinter unable to handle steep late-race climbs
